categoryComments.comments: Nice people
Good course
Nice
Great!
Great!
Great!

Sport - best 
Worst - no bad parts in loving it

Best - Atlantic World course 

Worse - very few contact hours

Best - high quality 

Worst - bit expensive
No bad aspects.
Great flexibility
Modern clean and sociable
lots of different areas to study, some busy and other quieter
good range of topics.
no fire exit on top floor flats. this was mentioned to unite students staff. nothing was done

- plenty of study spaces 
- subsidised prices in library cafe and union bar
- location into town is not to far 
- student support is strong
- sport and gym facilities are great
- some accommodations are abit dated

- lecturers are good and easy to approach with any issues or confusion  
- support with assessment is good
- some lectures can be less engaging than others
- contact hours are a good amount leaving rime to study

- halls are slightly dated and have experienced quite a few maintenance issues such as water leaks and power trips. 
- the ensuite rooms dont have good ventilation in the bathrooms. Alot of moisture gets trapped leading to a build up of mould and rust on door frame
- the community is good everyone is friendly
- hall committee and warden team are nice and really helpful
- catered meals can be hit and miss. Some days are better than others. Breakfast is always good though
- common room does need updating

reviews.courseDisplayName: History and Politics (with placement year) - BA (Hons)

categoryComments.comments: It is wonderful university, that provides a lot of opportunities for new student and supports their educational journey as well as sports and other aspirations.
Our course provides a lot of information and details on periods studied, as well as helps to develop necessary skills for understanding the subjects.
I like the location and the facilities, however I think that more control over the cleanliness should be enforced by the halls authorities.
The people are friendly and the lecturers and staff are extremely helpful
It’s straightforward
It’s great to take part in a variety of sport but if that isn’t your thing there isn’t massive else to do
A range of topics but very little contact time compared to other subjects
Very handy location
I like that it is a campus university, the town is nice as well. Sometimes feel things aren’t properly organised but that is more to do with the hall committee
There’s a boring module with a patronising lecturer but the others are good
Very conveniently located
It has been an amazing place to study. So welcoming and the town is a big student village. The staff are always happy to help and I cannot say I’ve felt more at home elsewhere.
It’s a great course. All modules are specific and it allows you to develop skills and learn what you are passionate about
It was homey, and I made a lot of friends who stuck with me throughout university.
the campus is beautiful, the staff and alumi are on it and all round best years of my life
Lectures are more than engaging and the opportunities for placements are endless
Best year of my life spent in my flat. I miss it dearly

It offers amazing opportunities for voluntary work, the facilities are maintained to a high standard. The union could make more of an effort to provide affordable services as well as make some improvements to the union clubs such as extending the last entry and mix up the themes and music played at the events

Lecturers offer their heart-led concerns for the students by making themselves available for help whilst maintaining a good level of teaching within the lectures. However I would wish for our Tutor group to have timetabled sessions as I have only seen my tutor and fellow classmates within the form only once in the past 3 months

Harry French is a decent hall situated in between town and the university. I cannot say I am extremely grateful for my placement as for one it was not my preferred choice of hall and two it is rather anti social with very little events occurring

Night life is dreadful however the campus, facilities, staff and Support network are all excellent. Loughborough town is one of the worst towns to live in within Leicestershire, however the scenic areas such as the outwoods, beacon hill and the University campus itself are lovely.
Have never seen my tutor in person
UTFE

The Uni is a very green and pretty campus. It is quite big and there are loads of sports fields and facilities. It is a fairly far walk from town (20ish minutes from the centre). But there are shops and bars on campus so its okay. 
The academic facilities are good and there is always access to WIFI, computers and study spaces. A lots of rooms are free for most of the day. 
There is lots of support for student both academically through your school and through the Student Union and wider Uni.
There are loads of clubs, societies and activities on campus all the time and everyone is friendly.

Course is taught in person but with few contact hours, around 10 a week. I would prefer more but this is substituted with lots of reading. 
The seminars are the best part where you get to talk and think with your peers. 
The lectures are interesting but are better when they include class discussion.

Really good value for money, cheap and food twice a day. Would 100% recommend. David Collet is the smallest halls on campus but is really friendly and has good social spaces, is right in the middle of campus and is run really well. All issues were sorted super quickly by the reception and management team. The heating and bill were all included so were cheap and the heating could be on all winter. We didn't have real ovens just these halogen lamp ovens but they cook stuff fine. All the hobs are induction hobs and they don't tell you so get induction pans. The WIFI worked all the time and was really quick. My hallway friends were really good but I heard other were really loud. Just great atmosphere and would suggest.

Loughborough Uni definitely has a great campus. there are plenty of different shops (etc) dotted across campus as well as a massive library. the LSU again holds a vast variety of different shops at relatively decent prices, the evenings at LSU are also decently priced as well as spacious, even on busy nights, with many different areas to go to throughout the night. Life at L'boro is definitely alright, pretty much in the centre of England, if you're looking to not go to Uni in a city here is ideal as it isn't too far away from a few cities like Nottingham or Leicester if you wanted to go on a night out there.

History (BA), like most degrees, is very hit and miss with content you find interesting. one benefit i would say about this module is that the exams for myself have either been coursework based or online long-window (23-hours) to complete a 1-2hour exam which is ideal it is very reading-heavy and they do ask a lot of you, literally tens of readings per week spread across the 4 modules per semester. and they do not offer a reading week which would be a God-send as The Atlantic World module is probably the heaviest when it comes to reading on top of the 3000-word coursework that is due about a month and a half into Sem1. personal tutors, from my experience, are great, my personal tutor is extremely approachable and he treats us as equals, which is very different from a levels where even though were young adults or even adults we are still children in the eyes of teachers, which is a very different from Uni.

The Holt is actually a cracking accommodation. for a decent price a small double bed with an en suite, as well as very polite staff. i would definitely recommend to people looking at Loughborough accommodation

when it comes to safety, we do have full insurance of our belongings under Unite Students, as well as CCTV pretty much everywhere externally, on top of key card activated doors to get into your block, flat, and bedroom 

being off campus, the wifi is sometimes a bit slow however, for the most part it works fine 

the choosing of accommodation as a whole at Loughborough uni could be improved, you can only pick accom from results day, compared to other unis where you can pick it from choosing it as your firm choice, and even then, it is broken down into different days, courses, gender, etc, meaning when it comes to your slot, if your category isn't close to the beginning, you may be left with the worse accoms.

It has had its ups and downs. I hated first year and feel I’d enjoy this year in different accommodation but my content is much more interesting and fun. I get to chose what I want to explore and delve into as well as having support for future endeavours

childQuestions.comments: They are really good. They have macs and computers for everyone to use.

The academic support is very good, they have accommodated to all of my issues which I’m thankful for. However, the uni seems to spend things on pointless things that could be invested in the mental health team, which lacks funding.
It really helped push what we wanted such as no more hard copies!

categoryComments.comments: Yes! So so close to town centre!
There are so many varieties and sports to stick into! Definitely something to accommodate for everyone

My accommodation is very expensive, is in a good location but is definitely lacking in safety. The boiler and water tank broke as did the plumbing which leaked into my room. The oven is old and struggles to work also
Fantastic Uni never regretted it a moment. Sports is world class. Student union puts on some fantastic nights and the Uni makes up for the average and small town

Loughborough is an amazing campus university with a buzzing atmosphere. You are spoilt for choice with sporting clubs and societies. The facilities for sports and teaching are first class and I have no negatives to say about the uni. The student union is a great place to go and overall the uni is just amazing.
